arts.ttl: Inter’s Calhanoglu reflects on derby loss
arts.sum: Inter midfielder Hakan Calhanoglu addressed the team’s recent derby defeat and their upcoming match against Udinese in a candid interview with Inter TV. The Turkish international emphasized the team’s determination to bounce back and return to their winning ways. “The loss against Milan certainly hurt us, because losing always hurts,” Calhanoglu admitted. “However, we’ve already put it behind us. This week we worked well, with intensity because we know our strength. Of course, we also know that it won’t be easy here in Udine, but we have good feelings because we worked very well this week.” When asked about what The post Inter’s Calhanoglu reflects on derby loss appeared first on FootItalia.com.

arts.ttl: Inter in Udine to go again after the derby
arts.sum: Inter have won six of their last seven matches against Udinese in Serie A (L1), scoring 19 goals in this period (2.7 per game); the Friulians` only success in this time came back in September 2022 (3-1 at the Blueenergy Stadium). After their 0-3 defeat against Roma, Udinese ended a nine-match unbeaten run in Serie A (W5 D4); the Friulians have not recorded two defeats in a row in Serie A since April (three, the first of which was against Inter).

arts.ttl: Inter’s defensive dilemma: Acerbi or De Vrij – Only one contract extension on the horizon
arts.sum: Inter Milan finds itself at a crossroads regarding the futures of two key defenders, Francesco Acerbi and Stefan De Vrij. With both players’ contracts set to expire on June 30, 2025, and the club’s new ownership, OakTree, looking to rejuvenate the squad, it appears that only one of these experienced center-backs will receive a contract extension. Tuttosport report that Acerbi, who will turn 37 next June, currently holds the starting position and has the advantage of being Italian, a factor that has historically been important to Inter’s management, particularly President Marotta. arts.ttl: Runjaic on Inter match: “We must perform in front of our fans”
arts.sum: Udinese head coach Kosta Runjaic addressed the media on Thursday ahead of his team’s upcoming Serie A clash against Inter Milan. The coach provided injury updates and shared his thoughts on the team’s preparation for the challenging fixture. Runjaic began by discussing the injury situation, confirming that Martin Payero will miss the Inter game due to a swollen foot. The extent of the injury is still unknown, with diagnostic tests scheduled for next week. The coach expressed hope that Payero might return after the international break.
